message:
So I'm mid-video call with my mom last week—she's showing me the new kitchen paint color—and BAM, the power cuts out. Total blackness. And of course my phone's at 12%. I'm fumbling around trying to find *anything* with a charge, and I'm thinking, "Why didn't I just plug in a power bank somewhere obvious?" 

Turns out there's nothing worse than being without light AND without the ability to check on people, get updates, or honestly just have a distraction while you're sitting in the dark. Since then I've stashed a little charged power bank in our kitchen drawer and a decent LED lantern on a shelf where we can grab it fast. No drama, just... there. 

It's funny how a 15-minute blackout reminds you that keeping your phone alive during an outage is just as important as actually having light. We found this handy breakdown on blackout basics really helpful for thinking through what actually matters.

What's your go-to device you'd be lost without if the power went out?
